key_issues: Item count is internally inconsistent: header (line 9) says '6 items' and the Why-this-batch prose (line 17) says barrel and test suite 'each earn separate items', but only 5 items exist (019-023) because item 021 bundles barrel + test suite. Reconcile the count with the actual item list.

Item 020 (in-memory adapter, M10) is not independently testable: it delivers the substantive async engineering but defers all behavioral tests to item 021, leaving only a module-loads smoke check as its acceptance gate. 020 can pass while async/ordering/close semantics are broken.

Item 020 deviates from the validated S2 house style, where every implementation item ships with its own tests in the same item (011/012/013/014/016). Move core behavioral tests (round-trip, async-delivery observation, on-close on both endpoints, on-error on induced failure) into 020; keep concurrency/stress + extra-info + ported fixtures in 021.
strengths: Scope is exactly Stage S3 (M6 + M10); no S4 leakage and no missing S3 deliverable.
Sequential numbering 019-023 continues cleanly from queue-002 item 018, no gaps or dupes.

Both architectural invariants captured as explicit acceptance criteria: async cross-thread delivery (send returns before peer on-message) and related-request-id defined-but-inert-until-S6a/M8 (do not strip).
Closeout references verified accurate against progress.md (acceptance boxes lines 106-111, Stage S3 status line 95, parity rows transport.ts/inMemory.ts line 110).
Correct dependency posture (L0 only) enforced by item 022 restricted-load portability test; granularity appropriate for a small stage (not padded).
